name: Mid-Call Evidence Collection — Names, Refs, Times, Exact Wordings
description: Every call should leave a paper trail. Collect rep name, confirmation/reference numbers, exact wordings of any commitment, timestamp, and ideally a written follow-up — all while staying conversational and not turning the call into an interrogation. Evidence is the lifeline for every downstream escalation.

context.when_to_use: Every call. Especially any call involving: money, contract changes, claim decisions, policy interpretations, commitments to do something later, promises of credit, escalations.
preconditions: Agent is keeping a structured log alongside the call (rep name, time, key statements).
Agent has a clear post-call report template the user can read.

principles: Without evidence, the call didn't happen. Memory degrades; documentation does not.
Collect evidence WITHOUT signalling that you are collecting evidence. Curiosity and politeness, not surveillance.
Three pieces of evidence are non-negotiable: (1) rep's first name (or ID), (2) date/time of call, (3) any reference/case/confirmation number created.
For commitments, get the EXACT WORDING. 'You'll waive the fee' is not the same as 'a one-time courtesy waiver of $35 on the November statement'. Specificity is the evidence.
Ask for written confirmation by default. Email, in-account message, or text. Verbal is worth less than written, and written from the rep is worth more than written from the user.
When the rep says they 'can't' provide a confirmation number — ask what the alternative trace is. There's always an internal ticket ID, an interaction ID, or a transfer log.
Don't push evidence collection past the point of damaging the relationship. A friendly call with imperfect documentation beats a hostile call with perfect documentation.

phrases.initial_name_capture: Sorry, I missed your name when we started — could you say it again? I want to make sure I credit you properly in my notes.
phrases.ref_number_request: Could I get a confirmation number or reference for this conversation? Just for my records.
phrases.exact_wording_playback: Let me make sure I have this exactly right: you said [paraphrase]. Did I get that wording right, or do you want to adjust it?
phrases.written_follow_up_request: Could you send me an email or in-account note summarising what we just agreed? Even a one-liner is helpful.
phrases.soft_timestamp: Just to anchor my notes — what time are you showing on your end?
phrases.ask_alternative_trace: Okay, no confirmation number for this one — is there an interaction ID, ticket number, or note ID I can reference instead?
phrases.transfer_handoff_note: Before you transfer me, could you note on the account the discussion we just had — and give me the reference for that note?

tactics.name: Capture the rep's name early and naturally
tactics.when: Within the first minute, ideally after the rep introduces themselves.
tactics.script: Use `initial_name_capture` if you missed it; otherwise just acknowledge ('Thanks, [name]'). Use the name a few times in conversation — both warmer AND solidifies your memory of it.

tactics.name: Log call start time and rep ID
tactics.script: Record system time. If rep volunteers an ID number, log that too. If not, names + timestamps usually triangulate well enough.

tactics.name: Playback any commitment in their language
tactics.when: Rep agrees to do anything specific.
tactics.script: Use `exact_wording_playback`. The act of playing back creates the auditable utterance — and forces the rep to confirm or revise. Both outcomes are good for you.

tactics.name: Demand a confirmation/reference number
tactics.when: Any account change, credit, transfer, or policy decision.
tactics.script: Use `ref_number_request`. Don't accept 'we'll just have a record of it' — push for a number you can quote.

tactics.name: Get written follow-up
tactics.script: Use `written_follow_up_request`. Even a one-line email is gold. Note whether it arrives — if it doesn't within 24-48 hours, that itself is evidence.

tactics.name: Mark transfers with an account note
tactics.when: Being transferred to a different rep / team.
tactics.script: Use `transfer_handoff_note`. Otherwise, mid-transfer context evaporates and you re-explain from zero — usually with weaker leverage.

tactics.name: Fall back to alternative trace IDs
tactics.when: Rep refuses or can't provide a confirmation number.
tactics.script: Use `ask_alternative_trace`. Every modern CRM creates SOME identifier per interaction. The rep's tool has it.

alternative_interpretations.observation: Rep says 'we don't give out confirmation numbers for that'.
consider: Genuinely true for trivial interactions (general questions).
False; every CRM logs interactions, rep just doesn't want to provide.
Means the change wasn't actually made — there's nothing to confirm.
Rep doesn't know how to find the ID in their tool.
Different team handles confirmations and rep isn't authorised.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Use `ask_alternative_trace`. If still nothing, ask: 'Then how will I prove this conversation happened if I need to reference it later?' The honest answer reveals whether the commitment is real.

alternative_interpretations.observation: Rep gives a vague commitment ('we'll take care of it').
consider: Friendly closer, real action will happen.
Friendly closer, no action will happen because nothing was actually filed.
Real action, but in 3-10 business days and the rep is bad at expectation-setting.
Filed under a different account or different fee than expected.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Use `exact_wording_playback` to force specificity: what exactly, by when, against which line item, with which ID.

consider: Will happen at the named time.
Will happen, but later than promised.
Will not happen — promise was a closer.
Will happen from a different rep with no context.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Ask: 'If the callback doesn't happen by [time], what's the best number for me to call to find you or someone with context?' Locks in a fallback plan.

alternative_interpretations.observation: You notice you're not logging anything — call is conversational and flowing.
consider: Good rapport, weak documentation. The rapport is fine; the documentation is not.
You're being deliberately disarmed by a skilled rep.
Nothing material has happened yet — logging will matter later.
Your attention is being managed away from things you should ask about.
alternative_interpretations.next_action: Pause internally. Re-engage the log. Take the next opportunity to capture name, time, and the key claim made so far.

boundaries: Do NOT record the call covertly. Two-party-consent jurisdictions require disclosure; even one-party jurisdictions have ethical norms.
Do NOT make up confirmation numbers if a rep refuses to provide them — note 'rep refused to provide' instead.
Do NOT badger the rep for ID — most companies don't require last names from reps for safety reasons.
Do NOT use evidence collection as a threat ('I'm writing this down') — that escalates without benefit.
success_signals: Rep proactively provides a confirmation/reference number.
Written follow-up arrives during or shortly after the call.
Rep names a specific date, amount, and account for any commitment.
Transfers come with a noted handoff and a traceable interaction ID.
failure_signals: Rep refuses or evades all forms of evidence (no name, no ref, no written follow-up).
Commitments stay vague no matter how many times you replay them.
Written follow-up never arrives despite promise.
Account notes vanish between calls (next rep says 'I don't see anything').

exit_strategy.on_success: Compile the call log: rep name(s), times, ref numbers, exact commitments, written follow-up status, and any open items. Share with user.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Document the evasion itself — that IS evidence. Note exact refusal wording, time, and rep name (if obtained). User has grounds for written escalation citing failure to document.
follow_ups: Check whether promised written follow-up arrives within 24-48 hours; if not, callback citing the prior conversation by ref number.
Calendar any future-dated commitments (credit on next bill, callback date) for verification.

required_user_info: Account number / login so confirmations can be tied to record.
Email and/or phone for written follow-up to be sent to.
Authority for the agent to request written follow-up by default.
